name: Sariel
description: Sariel, also spelled Suriel, is an archangel figure appearing in various Jewish and Christian apocryphal texts and esoteric traditions. Unlike some archangels with clearly defined roles, Sariel's attributes and functions vary across different sources, leading to some ambiguity and even negative portrayals. He is often associated with healing, particularly in some Kabbalistic traditions, where he is invoked for protection against illness. However, in other texts, such as the Book of Enoch, Sariel is listed as one of the fallen angels who taught humanity forbidden knowledge, leading to corruption and the eventual flood. This duality makes Sariel a complex figure, embodying both benevolent and potentially destructive forces. His connection to the moon and lunar cycles is also documented in some esoteric circles. The ambiguity surrounding Sariel's role warrants further exploration in comparative religious studies, examining the evolution of angelic figures and the interplay of good and evil within Abrahamic traditions.
subtitle: Angel of Healing and Knowledge

keyMyths.title: The Fall of Sariel
keyMyths.description: In some versions of the Book of Enoch, Sariel is depicted as one of the Grigori, or Watchers, who descended to Earth and cohabitated with human women. He is accused of teaching humanity forbidden knowledge, such as astrology and the use of herbs for potentially harmful purposes. This act of disobedience is seen as a catalyst for corruption and divine punishment, marking Sariel as a fallen angel and a source of earthly suffering. His actions lead to the great flood, cleansing the Earth of sin.

keyMyths.title: Sariel the Healer
keyMyths.description: Contrasting the Enochian narrative, Kabbalistic traditions often portray Sariel as an angel of healing. He is invoked in rituals and prayers for protection against illness and for the restoration of health. In this role, Sariel is seen as a benevolent force, working to alleviate suffering and to maintain the balance of the natural world. His association with healing is often linked to his connection with divine wisdom and understanding of the human body.

keyMyths.title: Sariel and Lunar Cycles
keyMyths.description: Some esoteric traditions associate Sariel with the moon and its cycles. This connection highlights the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. Sariel's influence is said to be strongest during certain lunar phases, making him a key figure in divination and magical practices. The moon's reflective light symbolizes Sariel's ability to illuminate hidden truths and to guide individuals through periods of darkness and uncertainty. This association further solidifies his role as a mediator between the divine and the mortal realms.

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
sources.work: A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen Angels
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of angels in Jewish and Christian traditions, including Sariel's various roles and associations.

associations.significance: Represents purification, transformation, and divine energy.

associations.significance: Sariel is often invoked for his healing abilities, particularly in Kabbalistic practices.

Represents completeness and divine perfection, often associated with archangels.

worshipPractices.name: Invocation
worshipPractices.description: In Kabbalistic and esoteric traditions, Sariel is invoked through prayers and rituals for healing, protection, and spiritual guidance. Specific incantations and meditations are used to connect with his energy.

cultural.modernLegacy: Sariel's presence in modern culture is primarily within esoteric and occult circles. He appears in angel therapy practices and is sometimes referenced in fictional works exploring angelic themes. His ambiguous nature makes him a compelling figure for those interested in the complexities of angelology.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Sariel's origins are shrouded in mystery, appearing primarily within apocryphal and esoteric texts rather than canonical religious scriptures. His name, meaning "God's Prince" or "Moon of God," hints at a divine connection and leadership role. Unlike archangels like Michael or Gabriel, Sariel's birth and introduction into the celestial hierarchy are not explicitly detailed in traditional narratives. He emerges as a figure already present within the angelic host, his attributes and functions gradually revealed through various mystical and theological interpretations. Some texts place him among the seven archangels, while others assign him a more ambiguous position, reflecting the diverse and evolving perceptions of this enigmatic figure. His association with healing and judgment suggests a complex role in the divine order, embodying both mercy and justice.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: Sariel's role in mythology is less about grand narratives and more about fragmented appearances across different texts. In some accounts, he is depicted as one of the angels who watches over humanity, tasked with observing their actions and reporting back to God. In the Book of Enoch, Sariel is mentioned among the angels who descended to Earth and engaged in forbidden unions with human women, leading to the birth of the Nephilim. This portrayal casts Sariel in a negative light, associating him with transgression and divine punishment. However, in other traditions, particularly within Kabbalah, Sariel is revered as a powerful healer and protector, invoked for his ability to cure illnesses and ward off evil spirits. This duality in his characterization makes Sariel a fascinating and complex figure in angelic lore, embodying both the potential for divine grace and the consequences of disobedience.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Sariel's legacy is primarily felt within esoteric and occult traditions, where he continues to be invoked for healing, protection, and spiritual guidance. His ambiguous nature, encompassing both benevolent and potentially malevolent aspects, makes him a compelling figure for those interested in exploring the complexities of angelic beings. In modern angel therapy practices, Sariel is sometimes called upon to assist with physical and emotional healing, offering comfort and guidance to those in need. His presence in popular culture is limited but can be found in fictional works that delve into angelic themes, often portraying him as a mysterious and powerful figure with a hidden agenda. While not as widely recognized as some of the more prominent archangels, Sariel's influence persists within niche spiritual communities, serving as a reminder of the diverse and multifaceted nature of angelic lore.

shortDescription: Sariel is an archangel in Abrahamic traditions, associated with healing and judgment, primarily appearing in apocryphal texts and esoteric traditions.
